intro:
I couldn't find much information on the plant "Eupritchardia thurstonii". However, I think you may be referring to "Euphorbia thurstonii", a species of succulent plant. 

Euphorbia thurstonii is a type of succulent plant, characterized by its unique, fleshy, and drought-resistant stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Euphorbia thurstonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
